preamble.summary: Judah marries and has sons. Er and Onan die. Tamar disguises herself and conceives twins by Judah — Perez and Zerah. Jubilees uses this to reinforce laws against prostitution.
preamble.remarkable: Jubilees retells Genesis 38 with a halakhic focus but preserves Judah's iconic admission: 'she is more righteous than I.'
preamble.friction: Judah's Canaanite marriage violates chapter 30's intermarriage prohibition. The tension is left unresolved.

verses.text_reference: And Judah took for Er a wife named Tamar, from the daughters of Aram.
verses.rendering: In the forty-fifth jubilee, Judah took a wife for his firstborn Er from the daughters of Aram. Her name was Tamar.
translator_notes: Jubilees specifies Tamar was Aramean, not Canaanite — perhaps to avoid the intermarriage problem.

verses.text_reference: But Er was wicked, and the Lord slew him.
verses.rendering: But Er was wicked, and the Lord put him to death. He left no children.

verses.text_reference: And Judah said to Onan: 'Perform the duty of a husband's brother.'
verses.rendering: Judah said to Onan: Go in to your brother's wife and perform the duty of a brother-in-law. Raise up offspring for your brother.

verses.text_reference: And Onan spilled it on the ground, and the Lord slew him also.
verses.rendering: Onan knew the offspring would not be counted as his. He spilled his seed on the ground. The Lord put him to death.

verses.text_reference: And Judah withheld Shelah from Tamar. And Bathshua died.
verses.rendering: Judah told Tamar to wait for Shelah, but Bat-shua would not let her son go to Tamar. Then Bat-shua died.

verses.text_reference: And Tamar put on a veil and sat at the gate to Timnath.
verses.rendering: Tamar took off her widow's garments, put on a veil, adorned herself, and sat at the entrance to the road leading to Timnath.

verses.text_reference: And Judah thought she was a harlot, and went in to her.
verses.rendering: When Judah passed by, he thought she was a prostitute. He went in to her.

verses.text_reference: And she said: 'Give me a pledge.' And he gave her his staff and signet.
verses.rendering: She said: Give me a pledge. He gave her his staff, cord, and signet ring. She conceived by him.

verses.text_reference: And Judah sent a kid but did not find her.
verses.rendering: Judah sent a young goat but could not find her. The local people said: There is no prostitute here.

verses.text_reference: And Judah was told Tamar was with child, and said: 'Bring her forth and burn her.'
verses.rendering: Judah was told Tamar was pregnant and said: Bring her out and burn her.
translator_notes: Judah's demand follows the priestly law of Leviticus 21:9.

verses.text_reference: And she sent the signet and staff to Judah.
verses.rendering: She sent the signet ring, cord, and staff to her father-in-law with the message: Identify whose these are.

verses.text_reference: And Judah said: 'Tamar is more righteous than I.'
verses.rendering: Judah recognized them and said: Tamar is more righteous than I, because I did not give her to my son Shelah. He did not lie with her again.
translator_notes: Jubilees preserves Judah's iconic admission from Genesis 38:26.

verses.text_reference: And for this reason she was not burned.
verses.rendering: For this reason she was not burned — because she had sought her rightful offspring from the line of Judah.

verses.text_reference: And it is written on the heavenly tablets: Do not burn any daughter of Israel for this.
verses.rendering: Because of this, it is written on the heavenly tablets: Do not burn any daughter of Israel for sexual immorality, but let her be judged according to the law.
translator_notes: This halakhic ruling limits the burning penalty to a priest's daughter only.

verses.text_reference: And Tamar bore Perez and Zerah.
verses.rendering: Tamar gave birth to two sons: Perez and Zerah.

verses.text_reference: And Judah acknowledged his sin and repented.
verses.rendering: Judah acknowledged his sin and said: I have sinned. He repented of this transgression.

verses.text_reference: And this sin was written on high until he made atonement.
verses.rendering: This sin was recorded on high against Judah, and forgiveness was not granted until he made atonement.

verses.text_reference: And this is ordained concerning all who commit fornication.
verses.rendering: This is the decree concerning all who commit sexual sin: they shall be brought to judgment.

verses.text_reference: And if any man commit this sin, he shall not be cleansed.
verses.rendering: If any man commits adultery and his sin is reported, he shall not be purified from his impurity.

verses.text_reference: And thus was the command concerning all fornication.
verses.rendering: Such is the commandment concerning all sexual immorality, for it is an abomination before the Lord.

verses.text_reference: And this iniquity shall not be expiated in the man's favour, but only in the woman's.
verses.rendering: This sin cannot be atoned for in the man's favor, but only in the woman's — for she acted because wrong had been done to her.
translator_notes: A remarkable qualification: Tamar's act is treated with leniency because she was denied her levirate right.

verses.text_reference: And we commanded Israel that there should be no uncleanness.
verses.rendering: We commanded the children of Israel that there must be no impurity among them.

verses.text_reference: For Israel is a holy nation.
verses.rendering: Guard against impurity in Israel. For Israel is a holy nation and a people set apart for the Lord.

verses.text_reference: And Judah mourned and was comforted.
verses.rendering: In the second year, Judah mourned for his wife. He went up to shear his sheep and found consolation.

verses.text_reference: And Shelah married, and Judah was blessed.
verses.rendering: Shelah married and had sons. Judah was blessed, and his sons grew and multiplied.

verses.text_reference: And Perez grew, and Hezron was born to him.
verses.rendering: Perez grew and was blessed. Hezron was born to him.
translator_notes: Hezron is the next link in the Davidic genealogy.

verses.text_reference: And Er and Onan died; Shelah, Perez, and Zerah lived.
verses.rendering: Er and Onan died, but Shelah, Perez, and Zerah lived, along with their descendants.

verses.text_reference: And all these things were written on the heavenly tablets.
verses.rendering: All these things were recorded in the books of Judah's generations on the heavenly tablets.
